destinations.evidence: Due Scheduler indices whose execution bitmap bit is set are suppressed before obligation emission.
destinations.snippet:
match is_executed(db, Some(scheduler), call_id, i) { Ok(true) => continue, Ok(false) => {}, Err(_) => continue }
obligations.push(ScheduledObligation { call_id, index: i, gas, num_calls, caller });

destinations.evidence: Bitmap helpers read the intended Scheduler execution state.
destinations.snippet:
pub const SCHEDULER_EXECUTED_BITMAP_SLOT ...
pub const SCHEDULER_EXECUTED_OVERFLOW_SLOT ...

destinations.evidence: Verifier consumes the already bitmap-filtered obligation set.
Builder-side helper uses the same suppression.
destinations.snippet: if matches!(is_executed(db, None, call.call_id, index), Ok(true)) { continue; }

equivalence_argument: Exact behavior is mapped: obligation enumeration filters every due (call_id,index) through on-chain executed bitmap state before builder/verifier consumers see it.

related_but_insufficient_code: Alloy and node repos contain related types/tests but not the obligation suppression owner.

notes: Mapped directly in scheduled verification and consumed by builder/verifier paths.
rationale: The primary candidate directly states that compute_obligations checks Scheduler executedBitmap through is_executed for each eligible call index and skips already fulfilled obligations. Its falsification condition also distinguishes the current behavior from a plain TTL-window or exact-block eligibility check.
statement: At current tip, compute_obligations must evaluate each eligible Scheduler callId/index in its TTL window against Scheduler executedBitmap and must not emit an obligation for an index that is already marked executed; clearing that executed bit should allow the otherwise eligible obligation to be emitted.
suggested_falsification_tests: Set executedBitmap for a callId/index before calling compute_obligations inside that index's TTL window and assert no obligation is emitted; clear the bit and assert the obligation appears.
Create two eligible indices for the same callId inside the TTL window, mark only one executed in Scheduler storage, and assert compute_obligations emits only the unexecuted index.
summary: Executed Scheduler call indices are suppressed from obligation emission in the port.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Set executedBitmap for an otherwise due (callId,index) and assert compute_obligations omits it; clear the bit and assert it is emitted.
title: Executed scheduler call indices are suppressed from obligations
